mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because rate cuts then transmit mainly through valuation rather than recession fears.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with declining FEDFUNDS, or if the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL inflation is flat to lower.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation stops falling, high policy restraint keeps real rates tight and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This mechanism is a valuation channel, not a growth channel.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3m annualized inflation rises or stays above its prior 6m trend
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals rising recession risk and deteriorating earnings expectations, which should pressure S&P 500 prices over the next week. The effect is strongest when PAYEMS is also weakening, confirming broad labor-market deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly signal windows do not show negative SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered cases, or if PAYEMS weakness does not materially improve the hit rate versus UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in PAYEMS indicates resilient payroll growth, which supports household income and corporate sales expectations. This should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month unless unemployment is simultaneously rising, in which case the payroll gain is likely temporary noise.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS-change regimes fail to outperform baseline by at least 0.5 percentage points over 20d, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and merchant revenues, which should lift near-term earnings expectations and push the S&P 500 higher over the next 60 trading days. The signal is strongest when HOUST is also improving, because housing-linked demand reinforces broad cyclicality.
failure_condition: Reject if a positive 3m trend in RSAFS does not lead to above-average 60d SPX returns, or if the combined RSAFS-HOUST signal does not outperform RSAFS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp downturn in HOUST reflects housing-cycle weakness that leads broader demand and credit conditions. When housing starts fall, equity returns over the next 20 days should be negative because cyclical growth expectations and risk appetite both deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6m moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend HOUST periods do not generate below-median 20d SPX returns, or if the hit rate is not at least 55% negative after threshold activation.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and operating leverage for cyclically exposed firms, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is most reliable when GDP is not contracting, because then higher production reflects real expansion rather than inventory restocking.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum fails to produce above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the signal is not stronger when GDPC1 is also expanding.

mechanism_narrative: A downward revision pattern in GDPC1 relative to earlier releases indicates demand softness that markets will eventually price into earnings expectations. In a slowing-growth regime, weaker real GDP should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDP vintage is below revised value
failure_condition: Reject if periods of weak GDPC1 growth do not lead to lower 60d SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if the relationship flips sign in growth slowdowns.
